question.text: Under LODR (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements), when must a listed entity appoint an RTA instead of managing the share registry in-house?

answer.text: When the entity has more than 1 lakh holders
When the entity has more than 10,000 holders
When the entity's market cap exceeds Rs.500 crore
All listed entities must mandatorily appoint an external RTA

explanation.text: **The Logic:** LODR allows listed entities to manage share registry in-house (requiring Category II RTA registration) or appoint an external RTA. However, entities with more than 1 lakh holders must appoint an RTA as in-house management becomes impractical. **The Trap:** Appointing an external RTA is not always mandatory -- small listed companies can manage in-house. The 1 lakh holder threshold triggers the mandatory external RTA appointment. **Cross-Exam Context:** This bridges NISM Series II-B (RTA operations) with NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) and corporate governance concepts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Most large listed companies use RTAs for their share registry. When clients hold shares of smaller companies, the registry quality may vary. For demat holdings through a DP, this is less of a concern as the depository provides standardized access.
